title: Discord Voice Bridge Settings
description: Settings metadata for the Discord Voice Bridge addon. Defaults live in settings.example.json; ignored local overrides and optional local test tokens live in settings.local.json.

fields.label: Enable Discord Voice Bridge
fields.help: Allows the addon to accept Discord voice turns and launch bridge instances when start-on-launch or manual Start Bridge is used.

fields.label: Auto-start bridge
fields.help: Compatibility auto-start flag. Start on NC launch is the preferred user-facing switch.

fields.label: Start on NC launch
fields.help: Starts the bundled Node bridge as soon as this addon initializes.

fields.label: Bridge mode
fields.help: Mock mode proves Discord transport. HTTP mode uses the real Discord Node bridge. TinyMVP mode uses a local fake voice-room bridge for testing without Discord.

fields.label: TinyMVP room URL
fields.help: Local TinyMVP fake voice-room server URL used when Bridge mode is TinyMVP.

fields.label: TinyMVP start with GUI
fields.help: When NC auto-starts the bundled TinyMVP room server, open its passive monitor window by default.

fields.label: TinyMVP bridge script
fields.help: Optional path to tiny_voice_bridge.py. Leave blank to use a TinyMVP folder next to the NC repo.

fields.label: TinyMVP poll interval (s)
fields.help: How often the TinyMVP bridge polls the fake voice-room state.

fields.label: TinyMVP NC microphone input
fields.help: When TinyMVP mode is active, use NC's local microphone as the human speaker and route accepted speech through the Discord bridge runtime.

fields.label: TinyMVP mic user ID
fields.help: Stable fake human participant ID used for local microphone speech in TinyMVP.

fields.label: TinyMVP mic user name
fields.help: Display name used for local microphone speech in TinyMVP.

fields.label: TinyMVP max phrase seconds
fields.help: Maximum phrase length for NC microphone input in TinyMVP mode.

fields.label: TinyMVP mic sample rate
fields.help: Sample rate for TinyMVP microphone WAV captures. 16000 Hz mono is usually enough for STT testing.

fields.label: TinyMVP mic device
fields.help: Optional microphone name fragment or index. Leave blank for the default microphone.

fields.label: Bot token environment variable
fields.help: Name of the environment variable containing the Discord bot token. The token itself should not be stored in session files.

fields.label: Local test token
fields.help: Optional write-only local token for private local testing. Prefer token_env_var for normal use; this value must remain in ignored local settings.

fields.label: Server ID
fields.help: Discord server/guild ID. Can be copied with Discord Developer Mode.

fields.label: Voice channel ID
fields.help: Discord voice channel ID to join.

fields.label: Allowed user ID
fields.help: Optional Discord user IDs. Use one ID or a comma/space separated list. Only used when Answer mode is allowed_user_only.

fields.label: Answer mode
fields.help: Controls whose voice turns can trigger NC replies.

fields.label: Silence cutoff (ms)
fields.help: How long Discord must be silent before a voice turn is considered complete.

fields.label: Minimum turn length (s)
fields.help: Captures shorter than this are ignored before STT/mock processing.

fields.label: Maximum turn length (s)
fields.help: Safety cap for one Discord voice turn. Use -1 for no hard cap; silence/idle detection still ends the turn.

fields.label: Bot maximum turn length (s)
fields.help: Safety cap for voice turns spoken by other Discord bots. Use -1 for no hard cap; this can be higher than human turns because TTS replies may be long.

fields.label: Bot idle finalize (ms)
fields.help: How long decoded bot audio must be idle before finalizing another bot's utterance. Higher values reduce premature bot-to-bot turn taking when TTS chunks pause between sentences.

fields.label: Ignore low-information transcripts
fields.help: Drops short Whisper hallucinations such as 'and' or '1' before they reach the chat provider.

fields.label: Low-information max duration (s)
fields.help: Only transcripts at or below this capture duration are checked against the low-information phrase list. Use 0 to apply the phrase list regardless of duration.

fields.label: Low-information transcript phrases
fields.help: Exact normalized transcripts to ignore when the capture is short. Keep real short answers such as 'no' out of this list.

fields.label: Saved capture WAV sample rate
fields.help: Sample rate for saved/STT capture WAV files. Discord is decoded at 48 kHz stereo, but 16 kHz mono is usually enough for speech recognition and keeps files smaller.

fields.label: Saved capture WAV channels
fields.help: Channel count for saved/STT capture WAV files. Mono is recommended for speech recognition and lower disk use.

fields.label: Use one shared capture owner
fields.help: When multiple NC Discord bots are in the same voice channel, only one active bot captures and forwards human speech to NC. Other bots remain available for speaking and routing.

fields.label: Capture owner timeout (s)
fields.help: How long the shared capture owner claim may live without a heartbeat before another active bot may take over.

fields.label: Save captured WAV files
fields.help: Keeps captured voice turns on disk for debugging the bridge.

fields.label: Play test tone on join
fields.help: Plays a short tone when the bot joins the channel to verify Discord output.

fields.label: Queue replies
fields.help: Serializes outgoing reply audio so Discord playback does not overlap.

fields.label: Interrupt reply on user speech
fields.help: Stops the current Discord reply, clears queued chunks, and aborts the active NC turn stream when the user starts speaking.

fields.label: Route protected mic speech
fields.help: When a moderator Current speaker is protected, still transcribe and route microphone speech into context without stopping playback or stealing the floor.

fields.label: Interrupt after continuous speech (s)
fields.help: Stops current reply audio after this many seconds of continuous user speech, even before STT confirms words. Use 0 to disable.

fields.label: Reply immunity window (s)
fields.help: Prevents immediate interruption during the first seconds of a bot reply. Continuous user speech can still interrupt once this window expires. Use 0 to disable.

fields.label: Discard bot speech on human intervention
fields.help: When a human starts speaking, active captures of other bots are discarded instead of being sent as partial bot-to-bot turns. This keeps human interruptions from cascading between multiple NC bots.

fields.label: Coordinate bot replies
fields.help: When multiple NC Discord bots answer the same room turn, the first bot with playable audio gets the floor and later simultaneous bot replies are discarded.

fields.label: Reply floor stale timeout (s)
fields.help: How long a shared bot reply-floor claim may live before another bot treats it as stale. This prevents crashed/stopped bots from holding the floor forever.

fields.label: Initial buffer chunks
fields.help: How many rendered TTS chunks are buffered before Discord playback starts. Lower values reduce latency; higher values reduce stutter risk.

fields.label: WAV max age (minutes)
fields.help: Deletes old Discord capture and reply WAV files after this many minutes. Use 0 to disable cleanup for debugging.

fields.label: Cleanup interval (minutes)
fields.help: How often the bridge checks for old scratch WAV files while it is running.

fields.label: Enable shared room router
fields.help: In multi-bot rooms, make one shared routing decision per Discord utterance before any individual bot generates a reply.

fields.label: Router mode
fields.help: LLM router asks the selected chat provider which bot, if any, should answer. Mention/question is a local fallback based on configured bot call names.

fields.label: Route to this bot when uncertain
fields.help: If the shared router fails or returns an unclear decision, allow the current bot to continue instead of staying silent.

fields.label: Route human speech to bots
fields.help: Allows human Discord utterances to be routed to a bot. Disable to ignore human speech at the shared room-router layer.

fields.label: Route bot speech to bots
fields.help: Allows one NC Discord bot's completed reply to be routed to another NC Discord bot.

fields.label: Exclude speaker from targets
fields.help: Removes the speaking bot from eligible routing targets before asking the router. This prevents most accidental self-routes.

fields.label: Route group bot invitations
fields.help: Allows invitations to multiple named bots, candidate bots, or the bot group to select one best bot instead of returning no route.

fields.label: Route open-room invitations
fields.help: Allows open invitations to the room or all participants to select one best non-human bot. Disable if open room talk should stay human-to-human unless a bot is named.

fields.label: Self-route policy
fields.help: Controls what happens if the router selects the same bot that spoke. Ignore blocks it, allow permits it, error blocks it with an explicit self-route reason.

fields.label: Uncertain fallback target
fields.help: When Answer when uncertain is enabled, choose whether uncertainty falls back to this bot, the first eligible candidate, or nobody.

fields.label: Router decision timeout (s)
fields.help: How long non-owner bot instances wait for the shared room routing decision file.

fields.label: Router decision max tokens
fields.help: Token budget for the small LLM router JSON decision. Reasoning-capable models may need more than the visible JSON because hidden reasoning can consume output tokens.

fields.label: Route grouping window (ms)
fields.help: Time bucket used to group the same utterance captured by multiple bot instances. Larger values are more tolerant of capture timing differences.

fields.label: Route bot replies from text
fields.help: After a bot finishes generating a reply, route the known reply text directly to the next bot instead of waiting for Discord audio capture and STT.

fields.label: Prepare routed bot replies ahead
fields.help: With LLM router, direct bot-text routing, and shared reply-floor coordination enabled, route a completed bot reply as soon as generation finishes so the selected next bot can generate/TTS while the current bot is still speaking. Playback still waits for the floor.

fields.label: Competing bot reply policy
fields.help: Policy label for how simultaneous bot replies should be handled. The current Node bridge implements first_ready_wins through the shared reply floor.

fields.label: Reply floor mode
fields.help: Policy label for the shared playback floor. first_ready_wins lets the first bot with playable audio speak while competitors are dropped.

fields.label: Enable moderator dead-air recovery
fields.help: When a completed routed turn selects no next speaker, let the selected Moderator bot step in.

fields.label: Dead-air recovery cooldown (s)
fields.help: Minimum time between automatic Moderator recovery turns. Use 0 for no cooldown.

fields.label: Dead-air silence timeout (s)
fields.help: Quiet-room grace period before automatic Moderator recovery runs. Use 0 only for immediate recovery after a no-route turn.

fields.label: Dead-air recovery trigger
fields.help: Choose whether dead-air recovery triggers only after bot speech or also after unrouted human speech.

fields.label: Dead-air recovery action
fields.help: Choose whether the Moderator speaks and hands off, speaks without handoff, or silently calls the next bot.

fields.label: Dead-air next-speaker strategy
fields.help: Choose how the recovery system selects the next bot after the Moderator bridge.

fields.label: Dead-air fallback target
fields.help: Optional bot id used by the selected-fallback next-speaker strategy.

fields.label: Routed text poll interval (ms)
fields.help: How often each bot process checks for text-routed turns addressed to it.

fields.label: Routed text max age (s)
fields.help: Discard text-routed bot turns older than this. Routed text is live handoff state, not saved conversation history.

fields.label: Router rules prompt
fields.help: Optional editable LLM router rules. Leave blank to use the transparent built-in default rules shown in the addon documentation/state.

fields.label: Discord persona system prompt
fields.help: Optional Discord-only personality instructions appended to, or replacing, the selected NC system prompt.

fields.label: Replace NC system prompt
fields.help: When enabled, the Discord persona prompt replaces the currently selected NC system prompt and NC visual/emotional instruction block for this bridge instance.

fields.label: Voice clone WAV
fields.help: Optional per-bot WAV reference voice. Relative names such as echo.wav are loaded from NC's root voices folder. Leave blank to use the voice selected in NC's global Persona tab.

fields.label: Context entries
fields.help: How many prior Discord history entries this bot sends to the chat provider. User and assistant messages each count as one entry. Use 0 to send all retained Discord history.

fields.label: Use selected RAG context
fields.help: When enabled, Discord turns may receive context from NC's active RAG Context addon if that addon is enabled and has matching chunks. This does not modify the RAG database.

fields.label: Persist room context between restarts
fields.help: When enabled, the Node bridge keeps its shared Discord room context JSON across bridge/app restarts. Leave off to start each bridge launch with a clean shared room context.

fields.label: Bot instances
fields.help: Optional array of bot override objects. Each enabled bot gets its own Discord token/channel settings, persona, localhost runtime port, Node process, and isolated chat history.

fields.label: Runtime host
fields.help: Local host for the addon runtime endpoint. Keep this on 127.0.0.1, localhost, or ::1 so the bridge is not exposed to the network.

fields.label: Runtime port
fields.help: Local runtime port for the single-bot setup. Multi-bot instances should each use a unique port.

fields.label: Allow non-localhost runtime host
fields.help: Advanced safety override. Leave off for normal use. When off, Start Bridge refuses runtime hosts outside loopback addresses such as 127.0.0.1, localhost, or ::1.

fields.label: NC bridge endpoint
fields.help: Local endpoint used by the Node bridge to send remote voice turns to NC.

fields.label: NC HTTP turn endpoint
fields.help: Local HTTP endpoint used by the Node bridge for Discord voice turns.

fields.label: Session mode
fields.help: Discord voice uses its own isolated context and does not append to the normal Chat tab history.

fields.label: Use selected STT
fields.help: Use the STT provider currently selected in Neural Companion for Discord speech transcription.

fields.label: Use selected chat provider
fields.help: Use the chat provider currently selected in Neural Companion for Discord replies.

fields.label: Use selected TTS
fields.help: Use the TTS provider currently selected in Neural Companion for Discord reply audio.
